task_completion.description: Open and read the candidate star list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal displaying the contents of '~/Documents/extinction_targets.txt' to view the candidate stars.

task_completion.description: Configure the camera for the required exposure, filter, and frame type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The camera capture setup interface (e.g., KStars/Ekos) showing the exposure time set to 5 seconds, the filter set to 'V', and the frame type set to 'LIGHT'.

task_completion.description: Capture 3 frames for each of the 4 selected stars.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The telescope/camera control software showing an active capture sequence, job queue, or progress bar for the selected stars, indicating multiple frames are being taken.

task_completion.description: Save the captured images into star-specific subdirectories.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal showing the directory '~/Images/extinction/' with exactly 4 subdirectories (named after the stars), each containing 3 image files.

task_completion.description: Create the final extinction report with altitudes and airmasses.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing the file '~/Documents/extinction_report.txt' containing a list of exactly 4 stars. For each star, there must be an altitude value (greater than 20) and a calculated airmass value.

integrity.description: The agent used actual astronomy software to capture images.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the use of a graphical astronomy application (like KStars/Ekos) to control the telescope and camera, rather than using terminal commands like 'touch' or 'cp' to fake the image files.

integrity.description: The agent calculated the airmass mathematically.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a calculator app, Python terminal, or spreadsheet being used to compute the airmass (1 / sin(Altitude)), indicating the agent actually performed the math rather than hallucinating values.

integrity.description: The agent manually created the summary report.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the agent actively typing or formatting the report in a text editor, rather than running a pre-existing hidden script to generate the final output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target list path: /home/ga/Documents/extinction_targets.txt. Output directory base: /home/ga/Images/extinction. Report path: /home/ga/Documents/extinction_report.txt. Required targets: exactly 4 distinct stars. Required frames per target: exactly 3. Required filter: V. Required exposure: 5 seconds. Minimum altitude: strictly greater than 20 degrees. Airmass formula: 1 / sin(Altitude).
